topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The ballroom air hummed with the sound of violins and effervescent laughter. I was nursing a lukewarm champagne flute, trying to appear engaged in a conversation with a distant cousin, when a familiar silhouette caught my eye across the glittering expanse of crystal and silk. It was him. Liam. He was standing near the entrance, his smile wide and effortless, a stark contrast to the guarded expression I’d practiced for the last hour. My breath hitched, a sudden, sharp intake that stole the air from my lungs. I felt a strange, dizzying lurch in my stomach, as if the carefully constructed composure I wore had just dissolved. I watched him move, a fluid grace that seemed entirely new, and my gaze snagged on the way the light caught the silver threads in his hair. I took a slow, involuntary step backward, my hand instinctively gripping the stem of the glass so tightly my knuckles turned white.

[story 2]

"Oh my God," I whispered, the sound barely audible over the orchestra swelling. My focus had been entirely on the dance floor, observing the swirling kaleidoscope of well-dressed guests, when I spotted her. Sarah. She was laughing, leaning into someone’s embrace, radiating a warmth that felt impossibly bright in the ambient glow of the chandeliers. Seeing her across the room, so vibrant and seemingly so happy, caused a strange stillness to descend over me. My feet seemed rooted to the marble floor. I found myself unable to move, just staring, cataloging every detail—the delicate curve of her smile, the way her dress flowed around her. A warmth spread through my chest, heavy and unfamiliar, making my skin feel suddenly too sensitive. I quickly looked away, forcing a shallow breath, trying to regain control before I simply stood there, paralyzed by the sheer presence of her joy.

[story 3]

The noise of the reception was a thick blanket, muffling everything except the sharp, insistent rhythm of my own pulse. I was attempting to navigate the crowded periphery, seeking a quiet corner, when I collided—not physically, but emotionally—with him. He was talking animatedly to an older gentleman, and for a split second, the world narrowed down to the space between us. His eyes met mine, and the easy familiarity that had once existed between us sparked something intense and unsettling. A wave of profound recognition washed over me, so potent it made my vision swim momentarily. I felt a strange lightness in my chest, a sudden urge to simply watch him, to absorb the entire scene, as if I were witnessing a perfect, unattainable spectacle. I managed a small, shaky nod, my throat suddenly dry, before swiftly pivoting and melting into the crowd, needing distance before I could unravel.
